DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO ENFORCE COURT ORDER RE: MOTION TO TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,140 IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION IS ESSENTIALLY A MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE COURT'S JUNE 23, 2017 ORDER COMPELLING PLAINTIFF TO PROVIDE VERIFIED SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES AND TO PAY MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUTN OF $350.00. PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 1008, THAT TYPE OF REQUEST IS A MOTION TO RECONSIDER, WHICH WAS REQUIRED TO BE FILED BEFORE THE SAME JUDGE WHO MADE THE JUNE 23, 2017 ORDER, WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THAT PRIOR COURT ORDER, AND BASED UPON NEW OR DIFFERENT FACTS, CIRCUMSTANCES OR LAW WARRANTING RELIEF FROM THE PRIOR COURT ORDER. THOSE REQUIREMENTS WERE NOT MET HERE. MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE APPROPRIATE TO COMPENSATE DEFENDANTS FOR THE COST OF MAKING THIS MOTION TO ENFORCE THE PRIOR COURT'S ORDER. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: MARK LEHOCKY; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; NOT REPORTED. (D302)

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Sohni Ahluwalia'S Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Terminating Sanctions - Plaintiff Sohni Ahluwalia's motion to set aside the February 7, 2018 order granting terminating sanctions is granted and she is required to pay defendants $9,582.50 no later than January 1, 2019, but failure to pay is not grounds for reconsideration of this motion. The February 7, 2018 order is now vacated and this case will be restored to the civil active list. Ms. Ahluwalia has shown good cause for granting this motion based on her attendance at the August 17 session of her deposition, her medical condition, and her apparent willingness to work with her counsel in an effort to move forward with this case productively. Granting the motion comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits and defendants will not suffer legal prejudice by the granting of the motion now that Ms. Ahluwalia has attended another session of her deposition. The amount sought by defendants for the work that their counsel did relating to the events leading up to the February 7 order is both reasonable and comports with the court's own observation of some of that work. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk

Off Calendar Feb-07-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Sonhi Ahluwalia, T:505.210.1010, plaintiff appearing on her own behalf;
Kenyon M. Young, Esq. of Kuluva Armijo & Garcia, T:415.273.6503.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on Defendants' Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or To Compel The Deposition Of Plaintiff Sohni Ahluwalia And For Monetary Sanctions as follows:

DEFENDANTS ANNE AND MATTHEW L'HERUREUX'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS is GRANTED. Ms. Ahluwala's repeated failure to attend her deposition without providing any admissible evidence supporting a medical or other excuse shows that she has no intention to sit for a deposition in this case. Under these circumstances, the prejudice to defendants from Ms. Ahluwala's discovery abuse cannot be remedied by anything short of terminating this case in favor of defendants. At the January 10, 2018 hearing the court stated that Ms. Ahluwala must make herself available for a deposition on a mutually agreeable date on or before February 2, 2018 or the motion for terminating sanctions will be granted. This was also stated in the order filed on the same date. Without legal justification, Ms. Ahluwala refused to attend her deposition that was scheduled for February 2, 2018. Since this case is being terminated in favor of defendants, it would be unnecessarily punitive to also grant defendants' request for monetary sanctions.

ORDER SIGNED in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.

Off Calendar Sep-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Kenyon M. Young of the law offices of Kuluva Armijo & Garcia (415-273-6503) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

Defendant stipulates to judge pro tem. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Defendants' Motion to Enforce Court Order Re: Motion to To Compel Plaintiff's Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, and Request for Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff in the Amount of $1,140 is granted. Plaintiff's opposition is essentially a motion for reconsideration of the Court's June 23, 2017 Order compelling Plaintiff to provide verified supplemental responses and to pay monetary sanctions in the amoutn of $350.00. Pursuant to CCP Section 1008, that type of request is a motion to reconsider, which was required to be filed before the same judge who made the June 23, 2017 Order, within ten (10) days of service of notice of entry of that prior Court Order, and based upon new or different facts, circumstances or law warranting relief from the prior Court Order. Those requirements were not met here. Monetary sanctions are appropriate to compensate Defendants for the cost of making this motion to enforce the prior Court's Order. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge Pro Tem: Mark LeHocky; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D302)
